title: Depth and breadth traversals

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Depth and breadth traversals; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Algorithmic Foundations; August 2023; material applicable to Depth and breadth traversals
sources.claim: Preorder, inorder, postorder, and level order visit tree nodes in different structural sequences suited to different tasks.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Preorder, inorder, postorder, and level order visit tree nodes in different structural sequences suited to different tasks.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Postorder processes both child subtrees before deleting or aggregating a node. Your goal is to choose, implement and test a traversal for a stated hierarchical task. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Recursive problem design; Linked lists; Tree terminology and representation; Stack ADT; Queue and circular buffer. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: Preorder, inorder, postorder, and level order visit tree nodes in different structural sequences suited to different tasks. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: In C, tree nodes and heap buffers need explicit lifetime and bounds; C++ can combine RAII nodes with vector heaps; Java uses object references and PriorityQueue contracts; Python uses objects plus heapq's min-heap functions, while the conceptual invariant stays unchanged.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Inorder has special meaning for binary trees, recursion depth can overflow, and iterative versions need explicit stack or queue state. The draft therefore rejects: All tree traversals produce the same order because they visit every node once.

includes: Implement preorder, inorder, postorder and level-order traversals iteratively or recursively.
Choose, implement and test a traversal for a stated hierarchical task.

In C, tree nodes and heap buffers need explicit lifetime and bounds; C++ can combine RAII nodes with vector heaps; Java uses object references and PriorityQueue contracts; Python uses objects plus heapq's min-heap functions, while the conceptual invariant stays unchanged.
excludes: Inorder has special meaning for binary trees, recursion depth can overflow, and iterative versions need explicit stack or queue state.

outcomes: Choose, implement and test a traversal for a stated hierarchical task.

Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Traversal order changes when the node action occurs relative to children; choose the order from the task.

examples.title: Depth and breadth traversals: implementation transfer

walkthrough: Step 1 — Keep the same specification and invariant across all four languages.

Step 2 — Map storage, library, equality, bounds, ownership, and error rules using: In C, tree nodes and heap buffers need explicit lifetime and bounds; C++ can combine RAII nodes with vector heaps; Java uses object references and PriorityQueue contracts; Python uses objects plus heapq's min-heap functions, while the conceptual invariant stays unchanged.
Step 3 — Retest this boundary in the selected implementation: Inorder has special meaning for binary trees, recursion depth can overflow, and iterative versions need explicit stack or queue state.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: All tree traversals produce the same order because they visit every node once.

misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use an invariant or counterexample to explain why this DSA belief fails: All tree traversals produce the same order because they visit every node once.

analogy.example: A carefully labeled physical arrangement can introduce Depth and breadth traversals, while the canonical invariant and operations remain the actual algorithmic model.
usefulFor: Introducing structure, order, connectivity, or state transition before formal notation
limitations: A physical arrangement does not reproduce asymptotic cost, aliasing, numeric limits, recursion depth, or the four languages' implementation rules.
